answer.text: Buying a call at a higher strike and selling a call at a lower strike (same expiry)
Buying a call at a lower strike and selling at a higher strike
Buying two put options at different strikes
Selling a call and buying a put at the same strike

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Bear call spread: Short call (lower strike) + Long call (higher strike). It's the opposite of a bull call spread. The trader is bearish and collects NET premium (lower call premium > higher call premium). **The Trap:** Bull call spread buys the LOWER strike call. Bear call spread SELLS the lower strike call. **Cross-Exam Context:** Bear spreads are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Bear call spread: Max profit = net premium received. Max loss = strike difference - net premium. It's a net premium receipt strategy.
